title: Clank & Slop — The Tape
tagline: — READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE —

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: Brent fell below $80 (about $79), the war premium still unwinding — but the Strait runs only ~12-15 transits a day against a ~120-140 norm, with roughly 500 ships still inside the Gulf. Open is not normal.

The US-Iran MOU still has no public text and has not gone to Congress; Vance calls it “a very general document.” Friday's Bürgenstock signing is the test of whether “signed” becomes a checkable instrument.
SpaceX options were the third-most-traded single-name line (1.8M contracts, calls 1.3-to-1) on a ~5% float — the reflexivity zone. The real supply test is the post-Q2 unlock, not the debut.
Anthropic's top models are dark a sixth day; the G7 floated “trusted-partner” access to advanced US models — frontier-model access hardening into an allied tier, the way chips already are.

props.title: Open on paper, idle at sea
props.subtitle: Strait of Hormuz vessel transits, per day · per Reuters
props.source: Reuters shipping-data reporting; pre-war norm per Reuters/Lloyd's List

items.why: Does an operative signed text appear, or does the 'very general document' stay a summary into the ceremony?
The formal US-Iran ceremony above Lake Lucerne — and the start line for 60-day nuclear talks.
Does the cutoff reverse via a US-person / trusted-partner carve-out, or harden into precedent?
Citi sees flow back by mid-to-late July; the desk watches transits, insurance, and mine-clearance, not the communiqué.
Whether the media fight moves from Washington to the states with a filed complaint.

items.what: Middle East crude slipped into discounts as the Iran deal lifted the supply outlook; the IEA sees a gradual Hormuz recovery tipping into a significant 2027 surplus.

The G7 trade-ministers' template — 'price-gap subsidies, joint procurement, quotas and price floors' for critical minerals — survived the summit, but the leaders' own declaration stayed conspicuously softer on who pays.
Senate Majority Leader Thune said Republicans are still trying to get the Iran text; an accord can be called 'signed' while the chamber that reviews sanctions and war powers hasn't seen a page.
